title: Linguistic Restriction
definition: The idea that language, by labeling concepts like 'time' and 'space', restricts our understanding of these abstract entities.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Our words can limit how we think about and perceive complex ideas such as time and space because they reduce them to simple, static labels.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The Sapir-Whorf Hypothesis suggests that our language shapes our perception of the world, including time and space, by providing categories and labels that constrain our thinking.
difficulty_levels.expert: By attaching words to abstract concepts, we inadvertently limit their comprehension to the bounds of these linguistic constructs. This leads to a disconnect between our conceptual knowledge and lived experiences, as philosopher Henri Bergson argued.

extra_fields.insight: Language may restrict our understanding of complex ideas like time and space due to the constraints imposed by labels.
